sourceMovie.title: Saltburn
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Oliver Quick's slow infiltration of a wealthy Oxford family's crumbling estate became the most talked-about film of 2023 — equal parts erotic thriller, class satire, and psychological horror, capped by an ending that reframes every scene before it.

recommendations.title: The Talented Mr. Ripley
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Matt Damon climbs the Italian social ladder with the same desperate hunger Oliver brings to the Catton estate — except Ripley's consequences are bloodier. The identity theft and obsessive desire to belong will feel like Saltburn's spiritual prequel.

recommendations.title: Parasite
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: A family infiltrates a wealthy household and the power dynamics curdle in exactly the way Oliver's summer at Saltburn does. Bong Joon-ho's tonal shifts between comedy and horror mirror Emerald Fennell's own refusal to pick a lane.

recommendations.title: Cruel Intentions
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Cruel Intentions (1999) laid groundwork for the tone and themes that Saltburn later expanded. If Saltburn resonated for you, this predecessor shows where that emotional DNA began.

recommendations.title: Call Me by Your Name
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: A summer guest becomes consumed by desire in a sun-drenched estate — the setting and obsession mirror Saltburn's golden-hour atmosphere before everything turns. Where Elio's longing is tender, Oliver Quick's is predatory, but both films make you feel the heat.

recommendations.title: The Killing of a Sacred Deer
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Yorgos Lanthimos traps a wealthy surgeon's family in the same suffocating dread that fills Saltburn's hallways. A mysterious outsider poisons a privileged household from within — the slow unraveling will feel sickeningly familiar.

recommendations.title: Phantom Thread
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Daniel Day-Lewis's couturier and his muse wage a war of control disguised as romance — the same obsessive power dynamic that drives Oliver and Felix. PTA shoots luxury with the same seductive eye Fennell uses on the Catton estate.

recommendations.title: Promising Young Woman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Emerald Fennell's first film shares Saltburn's candy-colored exterior hiding a razor underneath. Carey Mulligan's calculated performance has the same unnerving patience as Barry Keoghan's — you never know when the mask drops.

recommendations.title: Gone Girl
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Amy Dunne and Oliver Quick share the same sociopathic commitment to performance — both construct elaborate fictions to control the people around them. Fincher's cold precision makes every domestic scene feel like a crime scene, just like the Saltburn bathroom.

recommendations.title: Nocturnal Animals
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Tom Ford wraps revenge inside a dual narrative where wealth and aesthetics can't mask the ugliness underneath. The same tension between beautiful surfaces and dark interiors that makes Saltburn's estate feel like a gilded cage.

recommendations.title: The Riot Club
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Riot Club (2014) laid groundwork for the tone and themes that Saltburn later expanded. If Saltburn resonated for you, this predecessor shows where that emotional DNA began.

faq.question: What movie is most similar to Saltburn?
faq.answer: The Talented Mr. Ripley (1999) is the closest match — both follow an outsider who infiltrates a wealthy world through obsession and manipulation, with devastating consequences.

faq.question: Are there movies like Saltburn on Netflix?
faq.answer: Promising Young Woman and The Killing of a Sacred Deer are often available on streaming platforms. Check current availability as catalogs rotate monthly.

faq.question: What should I watch if I liked the dark tone of Saltburn?
faq.answer: Gone Girl, Nocturnal Animals, and Phantom Thread all share Saltburn's ability to make beautiful settings feel deeply unsettling.
